evals.name: basic-text-search
evals.prompt: Create a Vespa schema for a 'article' document type with fields: title (string, searchable), body (string, searchable with BM25 support), author (string, filterable), published_date (long, sortable), and url (string, stored only). Include a default fieldset combining title and body, and a basic BM25 rank profile. Write the schema to schemas/article.sd
evals.expected_output: A valid .sd schema file with correct field types, appropriate indexing pipelines (index for text fields, attribute for filter/sort), enable-bm25 on fields used in bm25() ranking, a fieldset, and a rank profile using bm25().

assertions.text: Attribute indexing used for filter/sort fields

evals.llm_rubric: Check this Vespa schema for best practices: (1) Do ALL fields used in bm25() ranking have 'index: enable-bm25'? This is critical. (2) Are filter fields (author) using 'attribute' indexing, not 'index'? (3) Does the url field use 'summary' only (stored, not searchable)? (4) Is published_date using 'attribute' for sortability? (5) Does the fieldset combine title and body?

evals.name: hybrid-search-embeddings
evals.prompt: I need a Vespa schema called 'product' for hybrid search. It should have: a title and description for text search with BM25, a 768-dimensional float embedding field with HNSW indexing using angular distance, and a hybrid rank profile that combines BM25 text scores with vector closeness in a second phase. The rank profile should accept a query embedding tensor as input. Write the schema to schemas/product.sd
evals.expected_output: Schema with string fields having enable-bm25, a tensor<float>(x[768]) field with HNSW config, distance-metric angular, and a rank profile with query input tensor, first-phase text scoring, second-phase combining normalize_linear of text and vector scores.

assertions.text: Query input tensor with matching dimensions

evals.llm_rubric: Evaluate this hybrid search schema: (1) Do text fields have both 'index' and 'enable-bm25'? (2) Is the tensor dimension consistent between the field and query input (both x[768])? (3) Does the rank profile properly combine BM25 and closeness scores with normalization in a second phase? (4) Are HNSW parameters reasonable (max-links-per-node 8-32, neighbors-to-explore 100-500)? (5) Is distance-metric explicitly set to angular?

evals.name: fix-broken-schema
evals.expected_output: Fixed schema file addressing: (1) schema/document name mismatch (products vs product), (2) match:exact on attribute-only field has no effect — needs index added, (3) tensor dimension name mismatch (d[384] vs x[384] in query), (4) missing distance-metric on embedding, (5) bm25(title) used without enable-bm25 on title field.

assertions.text: Tensor dimensions consistent between field and query

evals.llm_rubric: The original schema had these bugs: (1) schema name 'products' vs document name 'product' — must match, (2) 'match: exact' on attribute-only category field has no effect, (3) tensor dimension d[384] vs x[384] mismatch between field and query, (4) missing distance-metric on embedding, (5) bm25(title) without enable-bm25. Grade how many bugs were correctly identified and fixed. Also check the fix didn't introduce new bugs (e.g., adding 'index' to the tensor indexing pipeline is incorrect — tensors use HNSW, not inverted index).

evals.name: ecommerce-filtering
evals.prompt: Create a Vespa schema for an e-commerce product catalog. I need: product_id (unique identifier, fast lookup), name and description (full-text searchable), brand (filterable and searchable), price (numeric, filterable and sortable), categories as an array of strings (filterable), in_stock boolean (fast filter), rating (float), and a map of custom attributes (string to string, with filterable keys). Use appropriate attribute modifiers for performance. Write the schema to schemas/product.sd
evals.expected_output: Schema with product_id using attribute + fast-search, text fields with index, brand with index + attribute + fast-search, price with attribute + fast-search, categories as array<string> with attribute + fast-search, in_stock bool with attribute + fast-search + rank:filter, rating with attribute, map with struct-field indexing.
evals.llm_rubric: Evaluate this e-commerce schema: (1) Is product_id using attribute + fast-search for fast lookups? (2) Are text fields (name, description) using 'index' for full-text search? (3) Is brand using BOTH index and attribute (searchable AND filterable)? (4) Does in_stock use 'rank: filter' to skip scoring? (5) Are map struct-field keys indexed with 'match: exact'? (6) Is 'fast-search' applied to fields that need fast filtering (price, categories, in_stock, product_id)?

evals.name: parent-child-reference
evals.prompt: I'm building a Vespa app with two document types: 'merchant' (parent) with fields name, city, and trust_score; and 'offer' (child) with fields title, price, and a reference to the merchant. The offer schema should import the merchant's name and trust_score so I can use them in ranking without joins at query time. Create both schemas. Write merchant to schemas/merchant.sd and offer to schemas/offer.sd
evals.expected_output: Two .sd files: merchant schema with name/city/trust_score fields (attributes), and offer schema with reference<merchant> field (attribute-only), import field statements for merchant name and trust_score, and possibly a rank profile using the imported fields.
evals.llm_rubric: Evaluate these parent-child schemas: (1) Is the reference field in offer using attribute-only indexing (no index)? (2) Are import field statements correctly referencing through the reference field? (3) Are merchant fields that will be imported using 'attribute' indexing? (4) Does the offer schema have a rank profile that uses the imported fields? (5) Are schema and document names consistent within each file?
